headline_metric: Largest direct barrel exposure; Saudi alone 5.48 mb/d via Hormuz in 2024
global_or_regional_share: about 34% of global crude trade via Hormuz in 2025 per IEA/kmz.6
primary_sources: IEA Strait of Hormuz page; EIA Today in Energy 2025-06-16 figure data; EIA STEO accessed 2026-07-06
core_caveat: Actual removed vs delayed depends on bypass utilization and production shut-ins

headline_metric: No practical bypass for Qatar/UAE LNG; Qatar+UAE were 9.98 Bcf/d in 2024 and 11.43 Bcf/d in 1Q25
global_or_regional_share: almost one-fifth of global LNG trade; >25% of Asia LNG imports via Hormuz per IEA
primary_sources: IEA Middle East and Global Energy Markets accessed 2026-07-06; EIA LNG figure data; kmz.1 notes
core_caveat: Infrastructure damage and outage duration matter more than vessel rerouting

headline_metric: Middle distillates and jet fuel are a separate channel from crude
primary_sources: IEA Middle East and Global Energy Markets accessed 2026-07-06
core_caveat: IEA figure is Gulf exports; not all route exposure is public

headline_metric: Petrochemical and household-fuel feedstock shock; IEA says 30% of seaborne LPG exports crossed Hormuz in 2025

headline_metric: Overlooked fertilizer/acid input; IEA says about half of global seaborne sulphur trade moves through Hormuz

headline_metric: Gulf primary aluminium is a real Hormuz lane, not just an oil-adjacent story

headline_metric: Feedstock scarcity from LPG/naphtha losses is forcing polymer curtailments

headline_metric: Food-cost channel through Gulf nitrogen exporters

headline_metric: Shipping-risk premium multiplies every product row; peak war-risk quotes could exceed freight on some cargoes
